purpose: AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace covering a direct self-mailbox write-buf-one command that appends literal 1.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular self-mailbox write-buffer node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular self-mailbox write-buffer node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular self-mailbox write-buffer node.

layers.summary: A write-buf-one self-mailbox command appends literal 1 to the stem buffer and clears the mailbox source.
layers.boundary: This layer states direct self-mailbox write-buffer execution only; init-family commands, standard-signal commands, and recipient non-init command messages remain separate boundaries.

layers.summary: The self-mailbox write-buffer node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one self-mailbox write-buffer activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0161 direct mailbox write-buffer behavior and does not execute recipient command-message input.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested write-buffer command.
